_meta.notes: Second town west, between Cataract Mile and the Long Indices. The town is under a vow of silence — a self-imposed compact dating from a centuries-old plague where the only response to grief was to stop speaking. It worked, sort of, and the silence stuck. The vow-poppy fields ring the town; the petals are dried and sent to the Long Indices for ink-binding.

tagline: Nobody speaks here. They haven't for a long time.
ambientPool: The road into town has a hand-painted sign at the boundary asking visitors to honor the silence.
A poppy-cutter walks between rows; petals fall into a silk pouch without sound.
Two locals greet each other with a small bow and a nod; the weather is acknowledged in the angle of the chin.
A traveler from below the cloudline catches themselves about to speak and stops.

rooms.summary: A wooden post-and-lintel arch with the vow inscribed in five scripts on the lintel. Travelers pause here and most read the vow before crossing.
ambientPool: An elven Vellum reads the vow with the act fully active and crosses the threshold without dropping it.
A Topgrove orchardist on their way through stops to read the vow for the third time in their life.
A child of the village waits at the gate to take an arriving stranger's hand and lead them in.
The wind moves quietly through the arch. Everyone here listens to it more than they would elsewhere.

exits.narrative: Past the gate, along a path of fitted stones, into the poppy fields.

rooms.summary: Rows of vow-poppies, all white, all in slow steady bloom. Cutters walk between rows with silk pouches, gathering petals. Petals are paid by weight.
ambientPool: A cutter pauses, considers a particular bloom, and selects three petals.
A wagon waits at the field's edge to take the day's pouches to the Long Indices.
A bell-buoy somewhere in the next valley rings — the only sound from outside the silence.
The light is filtered through the poppies; everything is slightly warmer in tone.
